mission.title: rook: On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.
mission.description: On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.. This issue affects 43+ users.

steps.title: Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your rook deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
Is this a bug report or feature request?
* Bug Report

**Bug Report**

What happened:
A PV was attached to a POD. The node running the POD lost the power. Currently the status for the node is NotReady.

steps.title: Apply the fix for On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.
steps.description:
The status for the old pod is `terminating`. Does that pod never go away? Rook doesn't risk attaching to the new pod until the old pod is gone. 

 what if Rook goes ahead with the mount on [this line](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/pkg/daemon/agent/flexvolume/controller.go#L120) if the
```yaml
Warning  FailedMount  16m (x82 over 2h)  kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume "pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994" : mount command failed, status: Failure, reason: Rook: Mount volume failed: failed to attach volume pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994 for pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7. Volume is already attached by pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-zlhld. Status Running
  Warning  FailedMount  2m (x73 over 2h)   kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  Unable to mount volumes for pod "perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7_perfsonar(2394445f-1
```

steps.title: Confirm On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The status for the old pod is `terminating`. Does that pod never go away? Rook doesn't risk attaching to the new pod until the old pod is gone. 

 what if Rook goes ahead with the mount on [this line](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/pkg/daemon/agent/flexvolume/controller.go#L120) if the status is `terminating` and the reason is `NodeLost`?
